objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: breakout_donchian_adx
archetype: breakout_donchian_adx
family: breakout
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- donchian(period=25)
- adx(period=12)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close > donchian.upper and adx.adx > 30
- short: close < donchian.lower and adx.adx > 30
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, donchian.middle) or adx.adx < 15
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 1.75
tp_atr_mult: 6.0
description: ATR stop-loss (1.75x) and take-profit (6.0x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

iterations.hypothesis: Donchian breakout combined with ADX trend strength identifies channel breakouts during trend phases

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Key risks: false breakouts from range-bound markets, ADX lag masking recent trend shifts, potential whipsaws near channel edges. Validate trend alignment before entry signals.
[Backup plan] If ineffective: switch to RSI-based momentum confirmation, test shorter Donchian periods for quicker signals, or explore Bollinger Band breakout alternatives with standard deviation-based volatility filtering.

iterations.hypothesis: Donchian channel breakouts with ADX trend filtering captures genuine trend shifts while reducing false signals from pure breakout strategies

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Key weaknesses: false breakout signals near channel ends, potential whipsaws around ADX thresholds, and ATR-based risk parameters may not account for volatility changes. I'll monitor trade distribution and rejection rates at entry/exit rules.
[Backup plan] If current strategy underperforms: (1) switch to price action + volume confirmation, (2) test wider ADX thresholds (25/40), (3) use dynamic ATR trailing stops. Alternative approach: pivot to mean-reversion in range-bound markets using Bollinger Bands.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] Key weaknesses: potential whipsaws from tight bands, RSI overbought/oversold lag, ADX sensitivity. Risk/reward ratio may be suboptimal with current ATR multiples. Market context (1000CATUSDC) could have unique volatility characteristics.
[Backup plan] If current strategy underperforms: 1) Test wider Bollinger Bands (20->30 period) to reduce noise, 2) Evaluate dynamic RSI thresholds based on volatility, 3) Consider switching to mean-reversion approach using Bollinger Band crossover with tighter risk parameters.
